title: Sugar Dragon Goes to the Dentist

pages.alt: Sugar Dragon Goes to the Dentist. Front cover. A small, mischievous cartoon dragon with red scales, a yellow chest, and tatty grey wings grins at the reader.
Title page. Sugar Dragon Goes to the Dentist. Book 1 in the Sugar Dragon series. Less sugar. Less fillings. Healthy smiles.

pages.verse: Meet the SUGAR DRAGON, Red from horns to toes, With a chest of bright yellow And a soot-smeared nose.

pages.alt: The Sugar Dragon grins straight at the reader with a cheeky toothy smile. A curl of grey smoke rises from one nostril. Soft pastel sky behind him.

pages.verse: His teeth are all yellow, His wings are all grey. He puffs out black smoke When he's had a bad day. He has bags under his eyes Like a cat that won't sleep, 'Cause he's up half the night With the sweets that he keeps.

pages.alt: The Sugar Dragon sits on a heap of empty sweet wrappers in a moonlit attic den, looking tired and pleased with himself.

pages.verse: He LOVES fizzy cola, He LOVES chocolate cake, Lollies and toffee And fudge that you bake.

pages.alt: The Sugar Dragon dives headfirst into a giant pile of sweets, chocolate cake, lollipops, fudge cubes, and a tipping-over glass of cola with bubbles flying.

pages.verse: But he hates lovely water, He hates crunchy peas, And MINTY TOOTHPASTE Makes him wobble his knees.

pages.alt: The Sugar Dragon cowers with knees knocking, holding his nose. In front of him: a tall glass of water, a bowl of peas and broccoli, and a giant tube of toothpaste squirting out a fresh white minty blob.

pages.verse: There's one thing he loves Even more than a sweet: A rotten brown tooth He can sniff in the street.
He hollows it out, Then he curls up inside, And he flaps away home With the tooth as his ride.
But ZIP! Through the moonlight, Who's that in the sky? The TOOTH FAIRY catches him, "Oh no you don't, stay away! Goodbye!"

pages.alt: A small kind-faced Tooth Fairy with silver wings and a glittery lilac dress shines a bright golden torch beam onto the Sugar Dragon mid-flight. He shields his eyes guiltily. A rotten tooth tumbles from his claws.

pages.verse: Now meet little Maya, She's nearly aged three. She nibbles sweet sweeties while watching TV.
She slurps fizzy cola, She licks lollies flat, And she hides from her toothbrush Like it was a rat.
One morning, poor Maya Goes "OUCH!" with a yelp, Her front tooth is wobbly And asking for help.
The Sugar Dragon sniffs it From miles, miles away, "A ROTTEN ONE coming! HOORAY for today!"
But Mum says, "Oh Maya, Let's hop in the car. The dentist is friendly, She isn't too far."

pages.alt: Maya sits in her child car seat holding a cuddly toy, a small brave smile on her face. Through the window, the Sugar Dragon flaps frantically to keep up, recoiling from a road sign showing a giant tube of mint toothpaste.

pages.verse: "Hello there!" the dentist says, "hop in my chair. I'll count all your teeth And I'll tickle your hair."
"Open up wider. Say AAH, if you please." And Maya goes AAAH Like the breeze through the trees.
"Your wobbly one's normal, It's just on its way. A brand new big tooth Will pop up any day."
"But listen now, Maya, Here's my best tip: Keep SUGAR DRAGONS Well away from your lips!"
"Brush twice every day, Both morning and night. A pea-blob of toothpaste, Not too big, not too tight."
"Brush top, brush bottom, Brush round and around, For two little minutes Without too much sound."
So Maya brushed up When the morning sun rose, And again when the moon Tucked the stars in to doze.
The Sugar Dragon sniffed mint And he reeled and he gagged, "That HORRIBLE smell! My poor wings have gone ragged!"
Then PLOP went one wing, And PLOP went the other. "I'll have to walk home now! Oh BOTHER and BOTHER!"
The Tooth Fairy giggled, "Goodnight, sleep tight, Maya." And up rose the moon On a smile getting brighter. Less sugar. Less fillings. Healthy smiles.
